name: Neon Serverless Postgres
what_it_does: Neon is serverless Postgres with scale-to-zero and database branching. Agents can create instant database branches for each task, run queries, and scale to zero between uses — making it ideal for agent workflows that need isolated database environments per run, with full Postgres compatibility.
use_cases: Database branching for agent tasks — each agent run gets its own database branch
Scale-to-zero Postgres for agent workloads with bursty traffic patterns
Development/testing environments that auto-suspend when not in use
Agent-driven schema migrations tested on branches before main deployment
Serverless-first applications where database always-on is wasteful
not_for: High-frequency, always-on transactional workloads (cold start latency matters)
Very large databases (>100GB) where serverless overhead is significant
Applications requiring strict connection pooling guarantees
best_when: Your agent workloads are bursty or need isolated environments. Database branching is uniquely valuable for agent-driven CI/CD and testing.
avoid_when: You need always-on Postgres with no cold start latency, or you're already on managed Postgres (RDS, Cloud SQL) and the migration cost doesn't justify it.

auth.notes: Management API uses API keys or OAuth. Database connections use standard Postgres credentials + connection string. MCP server uses API key.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development. Pricing based on compute-hours + storage. Scale-to-zero means you don't pay when idle.

performance.rate_limits: 1000 concurrent connections on pooled; varies by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Postgres standard error codes plus Neon management API errors. Branch creation errors are clear. Connection errors include helpful context about cold start state.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Database operations follow Postgres semantics. Branch creation is idempotent with a given name. Migrations require careful idempotency design.
known_agent_gotchas: Cold start latency: scale-to-zero means first query after idle can take 1-5 seconds
Connection pooler (PgBouncer) required for serverless environments — transaction mode only
Branch storage is copy-on-write — large branches with many changes get expensive
WebSocket-based serverless driver has different behavior than standard Postgres drivers
Branch limits on free plan (10 branches) can be hit quickly in agent CI workflows
IP allowlist feature requires Business plan
agent_readiness.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, HIPAA, ISO 27001. Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). IP allowlisting on Business plan. Strong security posture for a startup.
